message:
feat(unicode): implement grapheme clustering and display width calculation

This commit adds Unicode text processing infrastructure that enables
correct handling of complex characters including emoji, combining marks,
and CJK ideographs in terminal rendering.

src/unicode/mod.rs - Module Exports:
- Re-exports grapheme and width functionality
- Public API: GraphemeInfo, WidthMethod, set_width_method
- Utility functions: grapheme_count(), display_width()

src/unicode/grapheme.rs - Grapheme Clustering:
- GraphemeInfo struct: byte_offset, byte_len, col_offset, width
- Uses unicode-segmentation crate for Extended Grapheme Clusters (EGC)
- Handles complex sequences correctly:
  - Emoji: 👨‍👩‍👧 = 1 grapheme (family ZWJ sequence)
  - Combining marks: é = 1 grapheme (e + combining acute)
  - Regional indicators: 🇺🇸 = 1 grapheme (flag)
- GraphemeIterator for efficient iteration over text
- Tab handling: width expands to next tab stop (configurable)
- grapheme_at_offset() for random access by byte position
- split_at_grapheme() for text splitting at cluster boundaries

src/unicode/width.rs - Display Width Calculation:
- WidthMethod enum:
  - WcWidth: POSIX-compatible, ambiguous chars = 1 (default)
  - Unicode: East Asian Width standard, ambiguous = 2
- Global width method: AtomicU8 for thread-safe configuration
- set_width_method() to configure default behavior
- char_width() returns display columns (0, 1, or 2):
  - ASCII/Latin: 1
  - CJK ideographs: 2
  - Fullwidth forms: 2
  - Emoji: 2 (typically)
  - Zero-width marks: 0
  - Control characters: 0
- string_width() calculates total display width
- Uses unicode-width crate for UAX #11 compliance

Design Decisions:
- Grapheme clustering essential for correct cursor movement
- Width calculation ensures proper cell allocation for wide chars
- Tab width is configurable (default 8) for editor compatibility
- Thread-safe global state enables runtime width method switching

Test Coverage:
- Grapheme counting for ASCII, emoji, combining chars
- Width calculation for CJK, fullwidth, control chars
- Tab expansion at various positions
- Edge cases: empty strings, single chars, mixed content

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
